auth_notes: Requires a Client ID and Client Secret obtained from the Delivery.com Developer Portal. Uses standard OAuth2 authorization code flow for user-level actions and API keys for merchant discovery.
rate_limits: Not explicitly documented for public tiers; typically requires developer agreement for high-volume production use.
gotchas: The API documentation is somewhat dated and may not reflect all current merchant constraints.
Order placement requires a complex multi-step cart creation and checkout flow.
Address validation is highly sensitive and often requires pre-processing to match the API's expected format.
research_notes: Implementation will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. You will need to set up a generic OAuth2 credential in n8n. Because there is no native webhook support, tracking order status changes will require a polling mechanism (e.g., a Cron trigger checking order status every X minutes).

Because there is no native webhook support, tracking order status changes will require a polling mechanism (e.g., a Cron trigger checking order status every X minutes).
